body: PT Kian Santang Muliatama Tbk (RGAS) has realized almost all of its initial public offering (IPO) proceeds of IDR 35.18 billion, with the remaining IDR 10.8 million placed in Bank Syariah Indonesia at an interest rate of 0.04%. The IPO proceeds were used for various purposes, including the acquisition of shares in PT Kian Santang, PT Karya Instrumindo Simpati, and Ergas Kians, as well as working capital for RGAS and its subsidiaries.
title: PT Kian Santang Muliatama Tbk Utilizes Almost Entire IPO Proceeds of IDR 35.18 Billion for Acquisitions and Working Capital.

body: Harimas Tunggal Perkasa's share percentage in PT Impack Pratama Tbk increased to 10.70 billion shares, or 19.73%, after acquiring an additional 468.75 million shares, while John Herry Teja became the new owner of 5.75% shares in PT Menn Teknologi Indonesia Tbk. Meanwhile, foreign investors dominated the biggest share sales, with HSBC-Fund Svs A/C Chengdong Investment Corp-Self selling 7.18 million shares of PT Bumi Resources Tbk and Morgan Stanley Co International Plc selling 7,000 shares of PT MD Pictures Tbk.
title: Multiple investors and companies engage in significant share transactions in various Indonesian companies, including manufacturers, service providers, and food chains.

body: PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) is terminating an unspecified number of employees as it shifts focus to exclusively virtual product services, ensuring fulfillment of all rights and compensation for affected employees according to applicable laws and regulations. Despite the layoffs, the company's stock price has shown growth, opening at IDR 114 and fluctuating upwards to IDR 115 by 2:00 pm WIB, indicating a positive market response to its business transformation strategy.
title: Bukalapak to terminate employees as it shifts focus to virtual product services, despite stock price growth.

body: PT Astra International Tbk (ASII) reported total car sales of 482,964 units in FY 2024, a 13.86% year-on-year decrease compared to FY 2023, but with a 6.97% month-to-month increase in December 2024. Brands under Astra's umbrella, including Toyota (TM) and Lexus, remained market leaders, with sales of 291,566 units, while Daihatsu and Isuzu saw declines, and UD Trucks and Peugeot recorded 1,960 units and 27 units, respectively.
title: Astra International reports 13.86% year-on-year decrease in car sales to 482,964 units in FY 2024, but sees positive trend in December sales.

body: Stocks of companies that recently held initial public offerings (IPOs) ranked among top gainers, with PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses Tbk (BKSU) leading the list with a 25% increase to IDR 5,075 per share, followed by PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk (RHEC) with a 24.66% increase to IDR 2,780 per share. Other top gainers included PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi Tbk (BRIG), PT Delta Giri Wacana Tbk (DGWA), and PT Raja Roti Cemerlang Tbk (RRCI), with increases ranging from 14.40% to 24.57%.
title: Newly listed Indonesian companies see significant stock price increases in first trading session.

body: PT Samcro Hyosung Adilestari Tbk (ACRO) and PT Manggung Polahraya Tbk (MANG) saw their stock prices drop 33.3% and 7%, respectively, since their IPOs, with ACRO's current stock price at Rp73 per share and MANG's at Rp93 per share. The two companies had raised Rp74.93 billion and Rp76.25 billion, respectively, during their IPOs, with ACRO offering 693.82 million shares at Rp108 per share and MANG offering 762.5 million shares at Rp100 per share.
title: PT Samcro Hyosung Adilestari and PT Manggung Polahraya's Stock Prices Decline After IPO.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (IHSG) opened lower on Monday, reaching 7,063.53 points, with large-cap stocks such as PT Bank Central Asia Tbk, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk, PT Bank Mandiri Tbk, and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk experiencing corrections. Coal stocks, including PT Bayan Resources Tbk, PT Bukit Asam Tbk, PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk, and PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia Tbk, also showed weakness during the morning session.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Opens Lower, Large-Cap and Coal Stocks Experience Correction.

body: PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi Tbk (OBAT) raised IDR 59.5 billion through its initial public offering, selling 170 million shares at IDR 350 per share, with the funds to be used for working capital, including raw material purchases, production expansion, and marketing development. The company's stock price surged 16.57% to IDR 408 per share on its debut, following an oversubscription of 45 times, with a promise to allocate up to 50% of post-IPO profits as dividends.
title: Indonesian pharmaceutical company PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi goes public with successful IPO, raising IDR 59.5 billion.

body: PT Surya Biru Murni Acetylene Tbk (SBMA) expects strategic projects in oil, gas, mining, and manufacturing sectors in the Kalimantan region to drive economic growth and increase demand for industrial gas, leading to revenue and profit growth. Despite strong financial results, SBMA's stock performance has been weak, with shares trading at IDR 120-122 per share, but the company remains optimistic about its future prospects, particularly in the medical gas sector, and aims to increase sales in 2025.
title: PT Surya Biru Murni Acetylene's business in Kalimantan region continues to grow driven by strategic projects in oil, gas, and mining sectors.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) has 19 companies in its initial public offering (IPO) pipeline, with at least 17 of them having large-scale assets valued at over IDR 250 billion, representing various sectors including non-cyclical consumer, basic materials, healthcare, and industrial. The remaining two companies have medium-scale assets ranging from IDR 50 billion to IDR 250 billion, with no specific company names mentioned.
title: Nineteen companies with large-scale assets are in the pipeline for initial public offerings on the Indonesia Stock Exchange.

body: PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses Tbk (CBDK) successfully held its initial public offering on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, raising IDR 2.3 trillion from the sale of 566.9 million shares at IDR 4,060 per share, with the IPO oversubscribed by approximately 344.2 times. The proceeds will be used to inject capital into its affiliate, PT Industri Pameran Nusantara, to finance the construction of a building for meetings, incentives, conferences, and exhibitions, with CBDK shares surging 25% to IDR 5,075 per share shortly after trading opened.
title: PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses Tbk Successfully Holds Initial Public Offering on Indonesia Stock Exchange

body: PT Indika Energy Tbk (INDY) has appointed MacMahon Holding Limited as the mining contractor for its gold mining project in Awak Mas, South Sulawesi, with a contract valued at AUD 463 million spanning seven years, with an option to extend for an additional five years. INDY has strong cash reserves of IDR 10.7 trillion as of September 30, 2024, and reported revenues of IDR 27.02 trillion and a net profit of IDR 521 billion for the first nine months of 2024.
title: PT Masmindo Dwi Area appoints Macmahon Holding Limited as mining contractor for its gold mining project in South Sulawesi.

body: PT Pertamina (PERT) is acquiring a 20% stake in Citicore Renewable Energy Corporation (CREC) for approximately USD 115 million, marking its first investment in the Philippines, and gaining access to several projects developed by CREC. The transaction, valued at PHP 6.69 billion, will allow CREC to expand its projects in Indonesia, and both companies will have opportunities for collaboration in the renewable energy sector.
title: Pertamina to Acquire 20% Stake in Philippines-Based Citicore Renewable Energy Corporation for $115 Million.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) fell 1.05% to 7,088.87, with average trading volume and daily transaction value declining 17.37% and 10.45%, respectively, over the past week. Top laggards included PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk and PT Bank Mandiri Tbk, while top contributors were PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk, PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Tbk, PT Petrosea Tbk, PT Barito Pacific Tbk, and PT Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua Tbk, which saw price increases of up to 8.01%.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Falls 1.05% as State-Owned Banks Weigh Down Performance.

body: PT Diamond Food Indonesia Tbk (DMND) was temporarily suspended from trading on the Indonesia Stock Exchange due to low liquidity, with daily average transaction volumes below 10,000 shares and daily average transaction values below IDR 5 million over the past three months. The company's share price has been volatile, ranging from IDR 765 to IDR 795 per share, with minimal transaction volumes, including a zero-transaction day on October 8, 2024.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Suspends Transactions of PT Diamond Food Indonesia Due to Low Liquidity.
